plugins.name: Snapshot on Event
plugins.description: Captures a high-quality still image whenever an event occurs.
plugins.value: Often more useful than full video clips for quick inspection.
plugins.comments: Extremely practical and lightweight. Strong complement to event recording. Minimal CPU cost, fully isolated within container.

plugins.name: Retention Policy Manager
plugins.description: Automatically deletes old clips, snapshots, or timelapses based on age or storage limits.
plugins.value: Prevents storage from filling up and keeps the system stable over time.
plugins.comments: Essential for real-world usage. Storage management is event-driven and deferred, minimal CPU impact. Fully isolated.

plugins.name: Snapshot Capture
plugins.description: Captures still images on interval, manual trigger, or event.
plugins.value: Simple and versatile capture method for many use cases.
plugins.comments: Highly flexible and useful across all scenarios. JPEG encoding adds minor CPU; interval-based means predictable overhead.

plugins.name: Motion Detection (Basic)
plugins.description: Detects motion using frame differencing.
plugins.value: Essential for security and automation use cases.
plugins.comments: Core motion detection. Efficient frame-differencing algorithm with reasonable CPU baseline. Essential ecosystem foundation.

plugins.name: Disk Space Guard
plugins.description: Monitors disk usage and warns or pauses recording when storage becomes critically low.
plugins.value: Prevents crashes and data loss due to full storage.
plugins.comments: Prevents catastrophic failure. Polling-based monitoring is lightweight; responds to storage events gracefully.

plugins.name: Thermal Watchdog
plugins.description: Monitors Raspberry Pi temperature and warns about thermal throttling.
plugins.value: Helps maintain system performance and reliability.
plugins.comments: Critical for Pi reliability. Temperature reading via /sys is standard container access, negligible CPU cost.

plugins.name: Metrics Dashboard
plugins.description: Displays FPS, latency, CPU usage, and stream diagnostics.
plugins.comments: Excellent for makers and developers. Metrics collection is deferred; UI rendering client-side minimizes server CPU.

plugins.name: Event-Based Recording
plugins.description: Records clips when motion or events are detected.
plugins.comments: Core use case. Encoding adds 5-10% CPU overhead only when recording. Storage-efficient and resource-aware.

plugins.name: Timelapse Generator
plugins.description: Creates timelapse videos from captured frames.
plugins.value: Great for hobby and creative use.
plugins.comments: Excellent for makers and workshops. Encoding is deferred/batch, so baseline CPU is unaffected. Very clean plugin boundary.

plugins.name: PIR Assist
plugins.description: Combines GPIO PIR sensor input with motion detection to reduce false positives.
plugins.value: Improves detection accuracy using physical sensors.
plugins.docker_note: Requires GPIO device mapping (standard Pi GPIO)
plugins.comments: Excellent for accuracy improvements. GPIO access via standard device mapping; event-driven polling has minimal CPU cost.

plugins.name: GPIO Trigger
plugins.description: Triggers events based on GPIO input (e.g. PIR sensor).
plugins.docker_note: Requires GPIO device mapping (standard Pi GPIO)
plugins.comments: Excellent for maker scenarios. GPIO polling is event-driven; standard Pi device mapping. Minimal CPU impact.

plugins.name: MQTT Publisher
plugins.description: Publishes events to an MQTT broker for home automation systems.
plugins.value: Enables integration with Home Assistant and other automation platforms.
plugins.comments: Strong ecosystem integration. Event publishing is lightweight; standard Docker networking. No device mapping overhead.

plugins.name: Webhook Notifications
plugins.description: Sends event data to external systems via webhooks.
plugins.comments: Highly extensible. Webhook calls are event-driven; standard Docker networking. Low CPU footprint, very clean boundaries.

plugins.name: Plugin Health Panel
plugins.description: Displays status and health of all enabled plugins.
plugins.value: Improves visibility and debugging of plugin system.
plugins.comments: Very useful for ecosystem maturity. Status polling is deferred; UI rendering client-side. Minimal server CPU overhead.

plugins.name: Event Timeline
plugins.description: Displays a chronological timeline of detected events with thumbnails and playback.
plugins.value: Makes reviewing footage intuitive and efficient.
plugins.comments: Strong UX improvement. Thumbnail generation is deferred; UI logic client-side. Low baseline CPU cost.

plugins.name: SMB / NAS Export
plugins.description: Saves recordings and snapshots to a local network share.
plugins.value: Provides local storage expansion without cloud dependency.
plugins.comments: Local-first aligned. Network I/O is asynchronous and deferred; standard Docker networking. Minimal baseline impact.

plugins.name: Motion Zones
plugins.description: Allows defining specific areas of the frame for motion detection.
plugins.comments: Practical control feature. Mask overlay adds per-frame processing cost (~3-5% CPU). Good workshop/doorway use case.

plugins.name: GIF Creator
plugins.description: Generates GIFs from clips or events.
plugins.comments: Fun feature but CPU-intensive. Encoding typically batched/async, so baseline streaming unaffected. Deferred processing.

plugins.name: Clip Buffer
plugins.description: Maintains a rolling buffer to capture moments before an event.
plugins.comments: Practical pre-event capture. Rolling buffer adds GC pressure (~2-3% CPU); consider object pooling to optimize.

plugins.name: Scheduler
plugins.description: Schedules when plugins or features are active.
plugins.comments: Very practical. Scheduling logic is lightweight; enables time-based noise reduction for workshops/pet monitoring.

plugins.name: Motion Detection (Advanced)
plugins.description: Background subtraction and noise filtering for more accurate motion detection.
plugins.comments: Good enhancement but CPU-expensive. Background subtraction adds 8-15% overhead. Good second-phase plugin.

plugins.name: Continuous Recording
plugins.description: Records the stream continuously.
plugins.comments: Storage/CPU intensive. Continuous encoding uses 8-12% CPU; storage-hungry on Pi. Consider selective recording instead.

plugins.name: Basic Authentication
plugins.description: Protects streams with username/password.
plugins.comments: Important for privacy. Authentication checks have negligible CPU cost. Consider whether core or plugin.

plugins.name: Edge Detection Filter
plugins.description: Applies edge detection to the video stream.
plugins.value: Useful for debugging and creative use cases.
plugins.comments: Debug/creative use only. Edge detection is very CPU-expensive (~20-30% overhead). Not recommended for Pi at scale.

plugins.name: Night Mode Filter
plugins.description: Enhances low-light video conditions.
plugins.comments: Useful for dark environments but CPU-heavy. Enhancement processing adds 15-25% overhead; may be hardware-sensitive.

plugins.name: HLS Streaming
plugins.description: Outputs the stream in HLS format.
plugins.comments: Web-friendly but CPU-intensive. Segmentation/encoding adds 8-15% overhead. Lower priority than direct streaming.

plugins.name: Cloudflare R2 Upload
plugins.description: Uploads recordings to Cloudflare R2 storage.
plugins.comments: Cloud storage conflicts with local-first philosophy. Credentials/trust concerns. Async uploads minimize CPU, but strategically weaker.

plugins.name: Telegram Notifications
plugins.description: Sends alerts via Telegram.
plugins.comments: Popular but external dependency. Event-based API calls have low CPU. Local-first fit weaker due to external service reliance.

plugins.name: Mio Reactive UI
plugins.description: Animates the mascot based on system state (idle, motion, alert).
plugins.comments: Branding/delight feature. Animation state updates are minimal; UI rendering client-side. Low practical value, high engagement potential.

plugins.name: Rules Engine
plugins.description: Allows combining conditions and actions (e.g. motion + person → notify).
plugins.comments: Powerful but risky. Complex state machines add CPU overhead and maintenance burden. Requires disciplined UX to avoid scope creep.

plugins.name: Person Detection
plugins.description: Detects human presence using lightweight AI models.
plugins.value: Adds intelligence beyond basic motion detection.
plugins.comments: AI inference is very CPU-expensive. 30-50% overhead per inference on Pi 4. Reliability concerns at scale. Better as later-phase enhancement.

plugins.name: WebRTC Streaming
plugins.description: Low-latency real-time streaming using WebRTC.
plugins.comments: Too complex for early phase. Real-time encoding + WebRTC overhead = 20-40% CPU. Very high maintenance burden; multiple failure modes.

plugins.name: Animal Detection
plugins.description: Detects animals (pets/wildlife) in the frame.
plugins.value: Useful for outdoor and hobby use cases.
plugins.comments: AI inference is prohibitively expensive. Same CPU concerns as person detection (~30-50% overhead). Narrower use case; defer to later phase.

plugins.name: Scene Description
plugins.description: Generates natural language descriptions of events in the scene.
plugins.comments: Fundamentally misaligned. LLM inference on Pi is unrealistic (~50-100% overhead, may require cloud). Breaks local-first philosophy.
